PortfolioOwner.title: PortfolioOwner
PortfolioOwner.description: Represents the individual who owns and manages the portfolio, containing their profile information and summary statistics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioOwner entity.

name.description: The full name of the portfolio owner.

aboutMeId.description: A brief biography or introductory text about the portfolio owner in Bahasa Indonesia.

aboutMeEn.description: A brief biography or introductory text about the portfolio owner in English.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the portfolio owner.

whatsAppNumber.description: WhatsApp contact number or a link to initiate a WhatsApp chat.

linkedInProfileUrl.description: URL to the portfolio owner's LinkedIn profile.

githubProfileUrl.description: URL to the portfolio owner's GitHub profile, showcasing code repositories.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the portfolio owner's profile picture or avatar.

totalProjectsCompleted.description: The total number of projects completed by the portfolio owner.

codingExperienceYears.description: The total duration of coding experience in years.

totalTechnologiesMastered.description: The count of distinct technologies or skills mastered by the portfolio owner.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a single project displayed in the portfolio, including details, media, and related skills/certificates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

titleId.description: The title of the project in Bahasa Indonesia.

titleEn.description: The title of the project in English.

shortDescriptionId.description: A brief summary of the project for list views in Bahasa Indonesia.

shortDescriptionEn.description: A brief summary of the project for list views in English.

fullDescriptionId.description: Detailed description of the project, covering problem, process, technologies, results, and impact in Bahasa Indonesia.

fullDescriptionEn.description: Detailed description of the project, covering problem, process, technologies, results, and impact in English.

demoUrl.description: URL to a live demonstration or deployed version of the project.

repositoryUrl.description: URL to the project's source code repository (e.g., GitHub).

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image or thumbnail representing the project.

additionalImageUrls.description: An array of URLs to additional images, screenshots, wireframes, or flow diagrams for the project.

category.description: The category of the project (e.g., 'Web Development', 'UI/UX Design', 'Backend Service').

isFeatured.description: A flag indicating if the project is one of the featured projects, to be displayed prominently.

skillIds.description: References to Skills used in this project. (Relationship: Skill N:N Project)

certificateIds.description: References to Certificates relevant to this project. (Relationship: Certificate N:N Project)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the project entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the project entry was last updated.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a technical skill or technology used in projects and mastered by the portfolio owner.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon image representing the skill, typically for display as a badge.

Certificate.title: Certificate
Certificate.description: Represents a certification obtained by the portfolio owner, which may be associated with specific projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Certificate entity.

titleId.description: The title or name of the certificate in Bahasa Indonesia.

titleEn.description: The title or name of the certificate in English.

issuer.description: The organization or institution that issued the certificate.

issueDate.description: The date when the certificate was issued.

credentialUrl.description: URL where the certificate can be verified or viewed online.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Represents a feedback or a quote from a client or collaborator, used to build trust.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

quoteId.description: The actual testimonial quote or feedback in Bahasa Indonesia.

quoteEn.description: The actual testimonial quote or feedback in English.

authorName.description: The name of the person who provided the testimonial.

authorTitle.description: The title, role, or company of the testimonial's author (e.g., 'CEO, CompanyX').

authorImageUrl.description: URL to the profile picture or avatar of the testimonial's author.

submissionDate.description: The date when the testimonial was received or published.

CareerTimelineEntry.title: CareerTimelineEntry
CareerTimelineEntry.description: Represents an event or period in the portfolio owner's learning and career journey.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CareerTimelineEntry entity.

titleId.description: The title of the timeline entry (e.g., 'Software Engineer at X Corp', 'Completed Bootcamp') in Bahasa Indonesia.

titleEn.description: The title of the timeline entry (e.g., 'Software Engineer at X Corp', 'Completed Bootcamp') in English.

descriptionId.description: A detailed description of the event or role in Bahasa Indonesia.

descriptionEn.description: A detailed description of the event or role in English.

startDate.description: The start date of the career event or period.

endDate.description: The end date of the career event or period. Can be null if ongoing.

type.description: The category of the timeline entry (e.g., 'Education', 'Work Experience', 'Personal Project', 'Milestone').

location.description: The geographical location associated with the entry (e.g., 'Jakarta, Indonesia').

UserPreference.title: UserPreference
UserPreference.description: Stores user-specific preferences such as theme and language, intended for client-side persistence (e.g., localStorage).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserPreference entity. For a single user application, this might be a fixed identifier like 'current_user_preference'.

themeName.description: The name of the currently selected dynamic theme (e.g., 'light', 'dark', 'blueModern').

languageCode.description: The ISO 639-1 code for the selected display language (e.g., 'id' for Bahasa Indonesia, 'en' for English).

definition.description: A singleton document representing the primary profile of the portfolio owner. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable.
A collection of all projects displayed in the portfolio. Includes references to skills and certificates. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable. Document ID is `projectId`.

params.name: projectId
params.description: Unique identifier for a Project document.

definition.description: A collection of all technical skills and technologies mastered by the portfolio owner. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable. Document ID is `skillId`.

params.name: skillId
params.description: Unique identifier for a Skill document.

definition.description: A collection of all certifications obtained by the portfolio owner. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable. Document ID is `certificateId`.

params.name: certificateId
params.description: Unique identifier for a Certificate document.

definition.description: A collection of testimonials or feedback from clients/collaborators. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable. Document ID is `testimonialId`.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: Unique identifier for a Testimonial document.

definition.description: A collection detailing the portfolio owner's career and learning journey. Publicly readable, admin-only writeable. Document ID is `timelineEntryId`.

params.name: timelineEntryId
params.description: Unique identifier for a CareerTimelineEntry document.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrator users. Only authenticated users whose `uid` matches an `adminId` document in this collection are authorized for write operations. Documents can be empty; their existence is the authorization signal. Document ID is the Firebase Authentication `uid` of the admin user.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an authorized administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for KaryaPro prioritizes public read access with authenticated admin write capabilities, adhering strictly to the core design principles. Since KaryaPro is a single-owner portfolio application, all public-facing content (PortfolioOwner profile, Projects, Skills, Certificates, Testimonials, CareerTimelineEntries) is designed for public readability, while write operations are restricted to a single, authenticated administrator.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by making all public data self-contained and not reliant on hierarchical lookups (`get()`) for read authorization. For write operations, the admin status is checked using a direct lookup on the `/admins/{adminId}` collection, ensuring the decision is atomic and doesn't depend on other document data or complex rule logic. This eliminates brittle dependencies and allows for atomic creation/update operations.

**QAPs (Query/Access Pattern Safety):** All main data collections (`/projects`, `/skills`, `/certificates`, `/testimonials`, `/careerTimelineEntries`) are designed as top-level collections. This structural segregation means that all documents within these collections share the same security posture (publicly readable). This inherently supports safe `list` queries, as no document needs to be filtered out for security reasons during a read operation; if a user can read the collection, they can read all documents in it. For admin write operations, the `/admins/{adminId}` collection ensures that the check for admin privileges is a simple existence check, not a query that could inadvertently reveal unauthorized information.

**Structural Segregation:** Each core entity type (Projects, Skills, etc.) is housed in its own top-level collection. This ensures that documents within a collection have a homogeneous security posture (publicly readable, admin writeable) and a clear, predictable schema. The `PortfolioOwner` entity, being a singular profile, is placed in a well-known singleton document `portfolioOwner/profile` rather than a collection, further enhancing clarity.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Public Data:** All primary content collections are globally readable. This is the simplest form of access model, directly supporting the public portfolio requirement.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** Admin access for write operations is managed through a dedicated top-level collection `/admins/{adminId}`. An authenticated user's `uid` must exist as a document ID in this collection to grant administrative privileges. This avoids custom claims and uses database content for role management.

**UserPreference Entity:** The `UserPreference` entity is explicitly excluded from Firestore paths as per the user's instructions that it's intended for client-side persistence (e.g., localStorage) without backend persistence.
